title: Oven Temperature Sensor Open Circuit
shortDescription: The oven temperature sensor (RTD/NTC) circuit is reading as open or disconnected.
fullDescription: Error E005 indicates the oven cavity temperature sensor is not providing a reading to the control board. The sensor wire is broken, the connector is disconnected, or the sensor element has failed open. Without temperature feedback, the oven cannot regulate heating.

causes.cause: Temperature sensor probe has failed
Sensor connector is loose or corroded
Sensor wire is broken (often near the oven cavity due to heat)
Control board sensor input has failed

symptoms: Oven may overheat if sensor fails mid-cycle
Temperature display shows error or dashes

fixSteps.title: Check sensor connection
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the oven away from the wall. Access the rear panel and locate the temperature sensor connector. Ensure it is firmly seated.

fixSteps.title: Test the temperature sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure resistance. At room temperature (77F/25C), an RTD sensor should read approximately 1080-1100 ohms. An NTC sensor reads 10-50k ohms. OL means the sensor is open.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the sensor wire
fixSteps.instruction: Check the wire from the sensor to the connector for damage, especially where it enters the oven cavity. Heat can degrade the insulation and break the conductor.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the single mounting screw inside the oven that holds the sensor probe. Pull the old sensor out through the back of the oven cavity. Feed the new sensor in and secure with the screw. Reconnect at the rear.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the sensor does not clear E005, the control board sensor input may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch oven E005 temperature sensor error fix

faq.q: Can I test the oven sensor without removing it?
faq.a: Yes. Disconnect the sensor connector at the back of the oven and measure resistance across the two pins. You do not need to remove the probe from inside the oven to test.

faq.q: What type of sensor does Bosch use?
faq.a: Most Bosch ovens use an RTD (Resistance Temperature Detector) type sensor. At room temperature, it reads approximately 1080-1100 ohms.

faq.q: Is E005 dangerous?
faq.a: If the sensor fails while the oven is heating, the oven may overheat before the safety thermostat activates. Do not continue using the oven with E005.

title: Oven Temperature Sensor Short Circuit
shortDescription: The oven temperature sensor is reading as shorted, providing a near-zero resistance reading.
fullDescription: Error E011 indicates the oven temperature sensor (or its wiring) has a short circuit. The control board reads near-zero resistance instead of the expected value. This can cause the oven to think the temperature is extremely high and refuse to heat, or in some cases, to heat uncontrollably.

causes.cause: Sensor probe has internally shorted
Sensor wires are touching each other (damaged insulation)
Moisture in the sensor connector causing a bridge
Sensor wire insulation degraded by heat

symptoms: Temperature display shows very high reading at room temperature
Oven may start heating and immediately shut off

fixSteps.title: Disconnect and inspect sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Access the sensor connector at the rear of the oven. Disconnect it and inspect for moisture, corrosion, or damage.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance across the disconnected sensor leads. At room temperature, it should read approximately 1080-1100 ohms. A reading near zero confirms a short.

fixSteps.title: Inspect sensor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor itself tests normal at the probe, the short may be in the wiring. Check for damaged insulation where wires pass through the oven wall or near heat sources.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the mounting screw inside the oven. Pull the sensor out, feed the new one in, and secure. Reconnect at the rear.

whenToCallPro: If the new sensor still shows a short, the wiring inside the oven cavity may need professional repair.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch oven E011 temperature sensor short circuit fix

faq.q: Can a shorted sensor cause overheating?
faq.a: A shorted sensor reads as very low resistance, which the board interprets as extremely high temperature. The oven should refuse to heat. However, some failure modes could theoretically cause issues, so repair promptly.

faq.q: Why do oven sensor wires short?
faq.a: The extreme heat inside the oven (especially during self-clean cycles at 900F+) degrades wire insulation over time, eventually allowing conductors to touch.

faq.q: Is E011 the same as E005?
faq.a: No. E005 is an open circuit (broken connection) while E011 is a short circuit (wires touching). Both involve the temperature sensor but are opposite electrical conditions.

title: Oven Overtemperature Condition
shortDescription: The oven has exceeded the maximum safe temperature during normal baking mode.
fullDescription: Error E030 is a safety alert indicating the oven cavity temperature has risen above the maximum allowed threshold for the selected cooking mode. During normal baking, the temperature should not exceed approximately 550F/290C. If it does, the control board shuts off the heating elements and displays E030. This can be caused by a faulty sensor reading too low, a stuck relay, or a genuine overheating condition.

causes.cause: Temperature sensor is reading too low, causing heater to run excessively
Heating element relay on the control board is stuck closed
Cooling fan has failed, causing heat buildup in the oven enclosure
Door seal is damaged allowing uneven heat distribution

symptoms: Food is burning despite correct temperature setting

fixSteps.title: Let the oven cool completely
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the oven and open the door slightly to allow cooling. Do not attempt any repair until the oven has cooled to room temperature.

fixSteps.title: Test the temperature sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Measure the sensor resistance. At room temperature it should read approximately 1080-1100 ohms. If it reads lower, the board thinks the oven is colder than it is and overheats.

fixSteps.title: Check the cooling fan
fixSteps.instruction: The oven has a cooling fan that prevents the enclosure from overheating. Listen for it during operation. If it does not run, check the fan motor and wiring.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door seal
fixSteps.instruction: Check the oven door gasket for tears, gaps, or compression. A damaged seal allows heat to escape unevenly and can trigger the overtemp sensor.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor tests fine, the control board relay may be stuck, requiring professional diagnosis. Also call a pro if you smell burning from the electronics area.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch oven E030 overtemperature overheating error fix

faq.q: Is E030 a fire hazard?
faq.a: E030 is actually a safety feature that prevents dangerous overheating. However, the underlying cause (such as a stuck relay) can be a fire risk. Repair promptly.

faq.q: Can I reset E030?
faq.a: After the oven cools, unplug for 5 minutes. The error should clear when power is restored. If the underlying cause is not fixed, E030 will return.

faq.q: What temperature triggers E030?
faq.a: In normal baking mode, E030 typically triggers above approximately 550-600F (290-315C). During self-clean, much higher temperatures are normal and do not trigger E030.

title: Oven Heating Timeout
shortDescription: The oven could not reach the set temperature within the maximum heating time.
fullDescription: Error E031 indicates the oven has been heating but could not reach the target temperature within the allotted time. The control board monitors how quickly the oven heats and triggers this error if the temperature rise is too slow, suggesting a faulty heating element, broken igniter (gas), or significant heat loss.

causes.cause: Bake or broil element has partially failed (electric)
Gas igniter is weak and not opening the gas valve (gas models)
Door seal is damaged, losing heat rapidly
Temperature sensor is reading too high, making board think oven is warmer than it is

symptoms: Oven temperature never reaches set point
Food undercooked after normal cooking time
Only one element (top or bottom) seems to work

fixSteps.title: Visual element check (electric)
fixSteps.instruction: Turn on the oven and watch the bake element at the bottom and broil element at top. Both should glow red when activated. If one does not glow or glows unevenly, it is failing.

fixSteps.title: Test elements (electric) or igniter (gas)
fixSteps.instruction: For electric: Disconnect power and test each element for resistance (20-50 ohms is normal). For gas: Check that the igniter glows bright orange and the gas valve opens. A weak igniter (drawing less than 3.2 amps) will not open the valve.

fixSteps.title: Check the door seal
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the door gasket around the entire perimeter. Look for gaps, tears, or areas where the seal does not contact the oven frame. Replace if compromised.

fixSteps.title: Replace the faulty component
fixSteps.instruction: For a failed element, disconnect wires and remove the mounting screws inside the oven. Install the new element. For a weak igniter, disconnect it and install a new one.

whenToCallPro: Gas igniter replacement involves working with the gas supply. If you are not comfortable with gas appliance repair, call a professional.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch oven E031 not heating element igniter fix

faq.q: Can a partially failed element cause E031?
faq.a: Yes. An element with a partial break may still produce some heat but not enough to reach the target temperature in time.

faq.q: How do I know if my gas igniter is weak?
faq.a: The igniter should glow bright orange and the gas should ignite within 60-90 seconds. If it glows but never lights, or takes more than 2 minutes, the igniter is weak.

faq.q: Why does only the broil element work?
faq.a: The bake element at the bottom may have failed. Check its resistance. In convection mode, the fan element may also be involved.

title: Convection Fan Motor Error
shortDescription: The convection fan motor is not operating or not reaching the expected speed.
fullDescription: Error E115 indicates the convection fan motor has failed or is not running at the correct speed. The convection fan circulates hot air inside the oven for even cooking. Without proper fan operation, convection cooking modes will not work correctly.

causes.cause: Convection fan motor has burned out
Fan blade is obstructed
Fan motor wiring is damaged by heat
Control board output for the fan has failed

symptoms: No air circulation sound during convection mode
Oven works in standard bake but not convection
Burning smell from motor area (behind rear oven wall)

fixSteps.title: Listen for the fan
fixSteps.instruction: Set the oven to a convection mode and listen. You should hear the fan running behind the back wall of the oven. If silent, the fan is not working.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the fan
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the rear panel inside the oven cavity (usually held by screws). Check the fan blade for obstructions. Try spinning the fan by hand. It should rotate freely.

fixSteps.title: Test the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the fan motor leads and measure resistance. A working motor typically reads 20-100 ohms. OL or very low readings indicate motor failure.

fixSteps.title: Replace the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the oven rear cover plate. Remove the fan blade (usually a single nut). Unbolt the motor from the rear wall and disconnect wiring. Install the new motor in reverse order.

whenToCallPro: If the fan motor is accessible and tests bad, DIY replacement is straightforward. Call a pro if you cannot access the motor or if the control board is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch oven E115 convection fan motor error fix

faq.q: Can I use the oven without the convection fan?
faq.a: Yes, standard bake and broil modes do not require the convection fan. However, convection cooking modes will not work properly until the fan is repaired.

faq.q: Why is my convection oven cooking unevenly?
faq.a: A failing convection fan cannot distribute heat evenly. Check for E115 or listen for the fan running. If it is slow or intermittent, the motor is wearing out.

faq.q: How long does a convection fan motor last?
faq.a: Typically 8-15 years. Excessive self-clean cycle use can accelerate motor wear due to extreme heat exposure.

title: Door Lock Error (Self-Clean)
shortDescription: The oven door lock mechanism has failed to engage for the self-clean cycle.
fullDescription: Error E302 indicates the door lock assembly cannot secure the oven door for the self-clean cycle. Self-clean mode heats the oven to extreme temperatures (900F+), and the door must be locked for safety. If the lock motor or switch fails, the self-clean cycle cannot begin.

causes.cause: Door lock motor has failed
Door lock switch is defective
Lock mechanism is jammed or stuck
Wiring to the lock assembly is damaged

symptoms: E302 error on display when starting self-clean
Door does not lock when self-clean is selected
Door locks but oven does not sense it locked
Clicking or grinding noise from lock area

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power for 5 minutes. The lock may have gotten stuck in an intermediate position. After power restoration, try the self-clean cycle again.

fixSteps.title: Manually check the lock
fixSteps.instruction: Look at the lock mechanism at the top front of the oven frame. Try to manually slide the lock back and forth. It should move freely. If it is stiff, clean and lubricate with high-temperature grease.

fixSteps.title: Test the lock motor and switch
fixSteps.instruction: Access the lock assembly from behind the control panel or top of the oven. Test the motor for resistance and the switch for continuity in both locked and unlocked positions.

fixSteps.title: Replace the lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the mounting screws, disconnect the wiring, and install the new lock assembly. Test by running a self-clean cycle.

whenToCallPro: If the lock assembly and wiring test fine, the control board may not be sending the lock command, requiring professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch oven E302 door lock self clean error fix

faq.q: Can I still bake with E302?
faq.a: Yes. E302 only affects the self-clean function. Normal baking, broiling, and convection modes do not require the door to be locked.

faq.q: Is the self-clean lock a safety feature?
faq.a: Yes. The lock prevents the door from being opened during self-clean when the oven reaches 900F+. Opening at those temperatures would be extremely dangerous.

faq.q: Can I clean the oven without self-clean?
faq.a: Yes. Use Bosch oven cleaner or a baking soda paste to clean the oven manually. Many professionals recommend manual cleaning over self-clean to reduce wear on components.

title: Door Lock Stuck in Locked Position
shortDescription: The oven door lock is stuck in the locked position and cannot release.
fullDescription: Error E303 indicates the door lock mechanism is stuck in the locked position. The control board has sent the unlock command but the lock switch still reports the door as locked. This typically happens after a self-clean cycle if the lock motor fails or the mechanism seizes at high temperature.

causes.cause: Lock motor has failed in the locked position
Lock mechanism is heat-distorted or seized
Lock switch is stuck closed
Power interruption during self-clean left lock engaged

symptoms: Oven is cool but door remains locked
Lock motor makes clicking sound but door stays locked

fixSteps.title: Wait for complete cooling
fixSteps.instruction: If the oven is still warm from self-clean, wait until it is completely cool (may take 1-2 hours). The lock is designed not to release until the oven is below a safe temperature.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle to reset lock
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power for 10 minutes. Reconnect and see if the lock motor activates and releases. Some models require starting then immediately canceling a self-clean cycle to trigger the unlock sequence.

fixSteps.title: Manual lock release
fixSteps.instruction: If the power cycle does not work, access the lock mechanism from the top of the oven or behind the control panel. Some models have a manual release lever that can be moved to unlock the door.

fixSteps.title: Replace the lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Once the door is open, replace the faulty lock assembly to prevent future occurrences.

whenToCallPro: If you cannot release the lock manually, a professional can safely disassemble the lock mechanism without damaging the oven.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch oven E303 door locked stuck self clean fix

faq.q: My oven door is locked and will not open. What do I do?
faq.a: First, make sure the oven is completely cool. Then try disconnecting power for 10 minutes. If still locked, look for a manual release lever behind the top of the oven frame.

faq.q: Can a power outage cause E303?
faq.a: Yes. If power is lost during a self-clean cycle, the lock may remain engaged. After power returns and the oven cools, try a power cycle to release the lock.

faq.q: How do I prevent E303?
faq.a: Do not run self-clean cycles during storms or periods of unreliable power. Consider reducing self-clean frequency to extend lock mechanism life.

title: Control Board Communication Error
shortDescription: The main control board has encountered an internal communication or processing error.
fullDescription: Error E305 indicates a communication or processing fault within the main electronic control board. This can be between the control board's processor and its memory, or between the main board and the relay board. This error prevents normal oven operation.

causes.cause: Power surge or spike corrupted board memory
Control board component failure
Loose ribbon cable between boards
Moisture damage to the control board

symptoms: Oven will not respond to any commands
Display may be blank or show garbled text

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power at the circuit breaker for 15 minutes. This performs a cold restart of the control board and may clear a corrupted memory state.

fixSteps.title: Check cable connections
fixSteps.instruction: Access the control board area behind the control panel. Check all ribbon cables and wire harnesses for secure connections. Reseat any loose connectors.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Visually inspect the control board for burned components, swollen capacitors, or signs of moisture. These indicate hardware failure.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If E305 persists after resets and all connections are good, the control board needs replacement. Transfer all connectors to the new board.

whenToCallPro: Control board replacement in an oven should be done carefully. If you are not comfortable working with the high-voltage wiring in an oven, call a professional.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch oven E305 control board error fix replacement

faq.q: Can a power reset fix E305?
faq.a: Sometimes. A 15-minute power reset clears many transient E305 errors. If it returns after reset, the board likely needs replacement.

faq.q: How much does a Bosch oven control board cost?
faq.a: Typically $150-$400 for the part. Professional installation adds $100-$200 in labor.

faq.q: Can I use the stove burners with E305 on a range/oven combo?
faq.a: If the oven and cooktop have separate controls, the cooktop may still work with an oven control board error. If they share a control board, both may be affected.
